description: Conduct a targeted demographic and housing vulnerability analysis to identify San Francisco zones with high concentrations of vulnerable senior households. Using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/aging_in_place.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, compute an 'Aging in Place Vulnerability Score' for zones with at least 20 senior households (age_of_head >= 65). The score is defined as the sum of the percentage of low-income senior households (income < 40000) and the percentage of overhoused senior households (households with <= 2 persons in buildings with 1 residential unit). Export the top 20 most vulnerable zones, sorted descending by score, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/senior_vulnerability_top20.csv including columns for zone_id, total_senior_hhs, pct_low_income, pct_overhoused, and vulnerability_score. Save a city-wide summary containing city_total_senior_hhs, city_pct_low_income_seniors, and city_pct_overhoused_seniors to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/senior_summary.json. Finally, generate a scatter plot of total senior households versus vulnerability score for all qualifying zones and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vulnerability_scatter.png. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
